ddls:
CREATE TABLE "providers" (
  "provider_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each provider */,
  "provider_name" TEXT /* Name of the provider */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the provider */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the provider */,
  "service_description" TEXT /* Description of the services provided by the provider */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the provider */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the provider */,
  "reliability_score" REAL /* Reliability score based on the provider's performance and uptime */,
  "security_policy_url" TEXT /* Link to the provider's security policy */,
  "support_phone" TEXT /* Phone number for support */,
  "support_hours" TEXT /* Support hours (e.g., 24/7, business hours) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("provider_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "resolver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resolver being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the resolver */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the resolver was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, query) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user accessing the resolver */,
  "query_type" TEXT /* Type of DNS query (e.g., A, AAAA, MX) */,
  "query_result" TEXT /* Result of the DNS query */,
  "query_duration" REAL /* Duration of the DNS query */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the access log */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the access log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, end-user) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the user account */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the user account */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON object describing the permissions associated with the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the role */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "resolver_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "resolver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resolver */,
  "metric_type" TEXT /* Type of metric (e.g., response_time, availability, security_score) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was recorded */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the metric entry */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the metric entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "security_incidents" (
  "incident_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each incident */,
  "resolver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the resolver */,
  "incident_type" TEXT /* Type of incident (e.g., DDoS, DNS poisoning) */,
  "incident_description" TEXT /* Description of the incident */,
  "incident_date" TEXT /* Date when the incident occurred */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the incident has been resolved */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Creation date of the incident log */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last update date of the incident log */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("incident_id")
)
